title: AHOL Tier 2 Variant Runner Return
description: Schema for the JSON object returned by an AHOL Tier 2 variant-runner to the Tier 1 orchestrator. A variant-runner spawns N task-runners (one per benchmark task), waits for them to finish, aggregates their results via SQLite, and returns this summary. Prose returns are a bug; callers must receive a dict that validates against this schema.

variant_id.description: Identifier of the prompt variant this runner evaluated. Must match either the canonical AHOL short form (V followed by digits, for example V7) or the long form (variant-<slug>, for example variant-baseline-01). Variant IDs are globally unique across rounds.

tasks_completed.description: Total number of benchmark tasks the variant-runner attempted and received a terminal result for (passed or failed). Does not include tasks that crashed the runner before returning.

tasks_passed.description: Number of benchmark tasks whose task-runner returned passed=true. Invariant: tasks_passed <= tasks_completed. The orchestrator computes pass_rate as tasks_passed / tasks_completed when tasks_completed > 0.

total_tokens.description: Sum of tokens_used across every task-runner spawned by this variant-runner, including runs that failed. Used by the cost gate.

wall_clock_sec.description: Wall-clock seconds from variant-runner start to return. Measures end-to-end variant latency including task-runner fan-out and aggregation.

error_log.description: Free-form error summary if the variant-runner encountered infrastructure problems (task-runners that never returned, SQLite write failures, container crashes). Null on a clean run. Task-level failures are NOT errors; they are captured in tasks_passed.

sqlite_row_ids.description: List of SQLite rowids the variant-runner wrote to the benchmarks database. Allows the orchestrator to reload the exact rows aggregated by this runner without relying on variant_id filtering.
